(HO1-4), Alam El Din et al. 2025 Comm. Bio. (HO5-8), Yuan et al. 2020 Nat. Comm. (Pr1-4), Bartram et al. 2024 eLife (Pr5-8). Newly recorded data: Murine organoids (MO) and Murine neonatal slices (MS)
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Two-photon calcium imaging of mouse PPC/PM during auditory virtual navigation (Funamizu et al. 2016)
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains session-wise NWB files for in vivo two-photon calcium imaging in head-fixed mice navigating an auditory virtual environment (Funamizu A, Kuhn B, Doya K (2016). Neural substrate of dynamic Bayesian inference in the cerebral cortex. Nature Neuroscience, 19, 1682-1689. https://doi.org/10.1038/nn.4390). Imaging targeted posterior parietal cortex (PPC) and adjacent posteromedial cortex (PM) across layers 2/3 and 5 while animals ran on a spherical treadmill in darkness with continuous or intermittent sound feedback. The dataset includes segmented ROI masks and population activity (raw ratio and ΔF/F) as RoiResponseSeries, together with aligned behavioral signals (locomotion/position, forward–lateral speed, lick rate, sound on/off indicators, and reward events) stored under the behavior module. Sampling is uniform at ~30.9 Hz for imaging and behavior, enabling trial-level analyses of neural dynamics during goal-directed navigation. Please cite the above article when using this dataset.
keywords: mouse, two-photon imaging, posterior parietal cortex, dynamic Bayesian inference

dandisets.title: Optogenetic disruption of theta-timescale spiking
dandisets.description: Rats transfected with a viral strategy to express a light-activated channel (ChR2) in PV+ neurons within the MS of PV-Cre transgenic rats. We also implanted an optic fiber targeting the MS as well as either silicon electrodes or multi-tetrode arrays targeting the hippocampus. Each of these animals received optical stimulation restricted to periods of locomotion on either a lineartrack, a W-shaped track, or both. The stimulation pattern was governed by a protocol that either activated MS PV neurons at a wide range of requested frequencies (“rhythmic stimulation”) or at specific phases of the ongoing theta rhythm (“phase-specific stimulation”) recorded in the hippocampus. Released in conjunction with the publication "Disruption of theta-timescale spiking impairs learning but spares hippocampal replay".
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Comparative anatomy of thermosensory projection neurons in Drosophila species
dandisets.description: Comparative neuron morphology dataset of thermosensory projection neurons (TPNs) from 2-photon microscopy data of dextran dye-filled neurons from Drosophila melanogaster, D. mojavensis, D. persimilis, and D. mettleri brains. In this project, we labelled the TPNs in the brain that relay heat information from the periphery (antennae) to the central brain (lateral horn and calyx of the mushroom body) through dextran dye filling. Included in this dataset are the following: Ten z-stack images of the labelled projection neuron called TPN-V (which is the main TPN for innocuous heat) per species, with five acquired in the "Front view" and five in the "Top view", respectively; Front and Top view fills of TPN-V for an additional population strain of each species; Representative fills of other TPN cell types (1a, 1b, and 6) labelled in D. mojavensis. See Capek, et al. (2025) for more details: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-08682-z.
species: Drosophila mojavensis wrigleyi - Drosophila mojavensis ssp. 'wrigleyi'

dandisets.title: Large-scale human intracranial electrophysiology and eye-tracking recordings from naturalistic image and movie stimuli
dandisets.description: This dataset includes 50 recording sessions of intracranial EEG and simultaneous eyetracking from 46 individual patients. Implants consist of a total of 10,769 contacts predominantly from depth electrodes, and 986 from ECoG contacts (grids and strips). Patients watched up to 53.6 minutes of movies (48 recordings), completed resting state (25 recordings) and viewed static images (31 recordings). Data is provided in NWB format and includes local field potentials, electrode locations, gaze and pupil size and precise timing of each frame in the movie. The data also includes annotations of words and phonemes in one of the movies, as well as annotions of faces in three movies.

dandisets.title: SUMMER: Dataset of human single-neuron activity during an 83-minute movie from 2,286 neurons and 29 patients
dandisets.description: SUMMER: Single Unit activity during a Movie in human Medial temporal lobe via Electrophysiological Recordings. This machine learning-ready dataset contains single neurons activity recorded from the medial temporal lobe of 29 intracranially implanted epilepsy patients as each patient watched the same full-length commercial film, "500 Days of Summer" (83 min. runtime). In addition to the 29 sessions, this dataset includes 53 frame-wise annotated movie features such as character presence, location, event boundaries, and main character speech. We also provide manually-reviewed electrode localizations, spike sorting quality metrics, and waveform information for each neuron.

dandisets.title: DataJoint fractal analysis workflow
dandisets.description: This repository contains an example NWB file containing the fractal dimension value calculated from a neuron in the MICrONS dataset in Boss DB.

dandisets.title: A brainstem map of orofacial rhythms
dandisets.description:
Data from "A brainstem map of orofacial rhythms." Heet Kaku, Liu D. Liu, Runbo Gao, Steven West, Song-Mao Liao, Arseny Finkelstein, David Kleinfeld, Alyse Thomas, Sri Laasya Tipparaju, Karel Svoboda, Nuo Li. BioRxiv 2025. doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.01.27.635041 

This dataset comprises Neuropixels electrophysiological recordings used to construct a brainstem-wide activity map in behaving mice, DeepLabCut-based tracking of orofacial features, and anatomical localization of recording sites registered to the Allen Mouse Brain Common Coordinate Framework (CCF). 

The corresponding analysis code is available at: https://github.com/NuoLiLabBCM/KakuEtAl2025

Sharing of this dataset was made possible by support from NIH DP1NS142432, R01NS131229, RF1NS132025, Pew Scholar Program, the McKnight Foundation, Simons Collaboration on the Global Brain.

dandisets.title: Disruption of theta-timescale spiking impairs learning but spares hippocampal replay
dandisets.description: Rats transfected with a viral strategy to express a light-activated channel (ChR2) in PV+ neurons within the MS of PV-Cre transgenic rats. We also implanted an optic fiber targeting the MS as well as either silicon electrodes or multi-tetrode arrays targeting the hippocampus. Each of these animals received optical stimulation restricted to periods of locomotion on either a lineartrack, a W-shaped track, or both. The stimulation pattern was governed by a protocol that either activated MS PV neurons at a wide range of requested frequencies (“rhythmic stimulation”) or at specific phases of the ongoing theta rhythm (“phase-specific stimulation”) recorded in the hippocampus. Released in conjunction with the publication "Disruption of theta-timescale spiking impairs learning but spares hippocampal replay".
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Contralesional activity reflects compensation, while brainstem detour pathways support skilled motor recovery after stroke
dandisets.description: Data for the publication "Contralesional activity reflects compensation, while brainstem detour pathways support skilled motor recovery after stroke"

dandisets.title: Pyramidal tract and corticostriatal neuron recordings in MPTP-treated macaque performing visuomotor tasks
dandisets.description:
Longitudinal single-unit electrophysiology recordings from the primary motor cortex of two rhesus macaque monkeys (Macaca mulatta), acquired both before and after induction of parkinsonism by systemic MPTP (1-methyl-4-phenyl-1,2,3,6-tetrahydropyridine) intoxication. Recordings span two subjects (Leu, recorded in 1993; Venus, recorded between 1999 and 2000), covering both the healthy baseline state and the parkinsonian state, and support analyses of how dopaminergic lesion alters cortical motor encoding.

Within primary motor cortex, two classes of cortical projection neurons are distinguished by antidromic activation from chronically implanted stimulating electrodes: pyramidal tract neurons (identified by stimulation from the cerebral peduncle) and corticostriatal neurons (identified by stimulation from the posterior putamen). Each recording site is verified as primary motor cortex by intracortical microstimulation at thresholds below 30 microamperes.

The behavioral paradigm is a visuomotor step-tracking task in which the monkey produces flexion or extension elbow movements against a torque motor to align a cursor with visual targets, through a structured sequence of center hold, target presentation, movement execution, target hold, and liquid reward. A subset of trials includes unexpected torque perturbations that elicit muscle-stretch reflexes, enabling analyses of sensory feedback in the parkinsonian state.

Original analyses on subsets of these data appear in three peer-reviewed papers (Pasquereau and Turner 2011, Cerebral Cortex; Pasquereau and Turner 2013, Frontiers in Systems Neuroscience; Pasquereau, DeLong, and Turner 2016, Brain), linked in the related-resources section.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: OpenScope Predictive Processing Community - Ecephys
dandisets.description: This dataset contains data acquired on the Ecephys platform as part of the OpenScope predictive processing community project
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: mouse PPC tetrode recording
dandisets.description: mouse PPC tetrode recording in an auditory probabilistic decision-making task
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Neural activity in rat nucleus accumbens and ventral pallidum during probabilistic free-choice decision-making (Ito & Doya 2009)
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains NWB files with behavioral and extracellular electrophysiological recordings from six male Long-Evans rats performing a conditional free-choice task with probabilistic rewards. In the task, rats maintained a center nose-poke during an auditory cue and then chose left or right nose-pokes; reward probabilities for each action switched across blocks according to four (left, right) schedules: (90,50), (50,90), (50,10), and (10,50) percent. Single-unit activity was recorded using movable microwire bundles implanted in the nucleus accumbens and ventral pallidum across 70 sessions (≈60,000 trials in total). For each trial, the dataset includes spike times, trial type (choice vs no-choice), selected action, reward outcome, and key event timestamps (tones, nose-pokes, pellet retrieval), all aligned within session time. These data were originally collected for Ito & Doya, J Neurosci 2009 (DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.6157-08.2009), which compared reinforcement-learning models of behavior and analyzed how action values, trial type, and reward outcome are represented in ventral basal ganglia. This dataset is suitable for re-analysis of decision-making strategies, model-based neural coding, and computational studies of cortico-basal ganglia circuits.

dandisets.title: Neural activities in dorsolateral, dorsomedial, and ventral striatum during fixed- and free-choice tasks in rats (Ito & Doya 2015)
dandisets.description:
This Dandiset contains Neurodata Without Borders (NWB) files with behavioral and extracellular electrophysiological recordings from seven male Long–Evans rats performing fixed-choice and free-choice nose-poke tasks with probabilistic rewards. In each trial, rats initiated a center nose-poke, received one of three cue tones (left, right, or choice), maintained the poke during the cue, and then chose the left or right port; sucrose pellets were delivered according to cue- and action-dependent reward probabilities. For left and right cues, reward probabilities were fixed at (left, right) = (50%, 0%) and (0%, 50%), respectively, whereas for the choice cue they varied blockwise across four schedules: (90%, 50%), (50%, 90%), (50%, 10%), and (10%, 50%). 
Neural activity was recorded with movable microwire bundles implanted in dorsolateral striatum (DLS), dorsomedial striatum (DMS), and ventral striatum (VS), yielding phasically active neurons (putative medium spiny neurons) from each subregion across 86 sessions and ~78,000 trials. For each session, the NWB files provide spike times (and associated unit metadata where available) together with detailed behavioral event timestamps (tone onsets/offsets, nose-poke entries/exits, reward/no-reward tones, pellet detection), block type (single fixed-choice, double fixed-choice, free-choice, extinction), cue identity, chosen action, and reward outcome, all aligned in session time. These data were originally collected for the Journal of Neuroscience article “Distinct neural representation in the dorsolateral, dorsomedial, and ventral parts of the striatum during fixed- and free-choice tasks” (Ito & Doya, 2015, J Neurosci 35(8):3499–3514, DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.1962-14.2015), which analyzed how state value, action value, reward outcome, and action commands are differentially represented across striatal subregions. This dataset enables re-analysis of decision-making strategies, model-based and model-free value coding, and hierarchical reinforcement-learning hypotheses in cortico-basal ganglia circuits.

dandisets.title: Months-long stability of the head-direction system
dandisets.description: This Dandiset includes longitudinal 1-photon imaging (UCLA miniscope) recordings in the Postsubiculum of freely-moving mice. All sessions are part of a longitudinal experiment: one month in a single environment, two months where the environment changes every two weeks, random visits of four environments over four weeks, and sparse explorations of two environments over five weeks.  The Dandiset also includes electrophysiological recordings from four animals exploring three enviroments in a single day.

dandisets.title: Multi-electrode array recordings of rat retinal ganglion cells with LED stimulation for retinotectal cell identification via ReaChR phototagging
dandisets.description:
Retinal tissue was obtained from Long-Evans rats that received stereotactic injections of AAV2retro-CAG-ReaChR-mCitrine into the superior colliculus. This retrograde viral approach resulted in expression of the red-shifted channelrhodopsin ReaChR and the fluorophore mCitrine in retinotectal RGCs.
Ex vivo retinal recordings were performed on 512-electrode arrays following pharmacological blockade of photoreceptor-mediated responses using L-AP4, DL-AP5, and CNQX. LED flashes at 565 nm were delivered at multiple intensities and durations (10, 30, 100, and 200 ms) to selectively activate ReaChR-expressing cells. The dataset contains recordings from 6 experiments, each capturing responses from hundreds of RGCs to the LED stimulation protocol.

dandisets.title: Data for parametrizing alternating current stimulation for neuromodulation (Bell Vila, M., et al, 2026)
dandisets.description:
Data collected using a 4x4 multielectrode array (Microprobes) in propofol anesthetized rats receiving subcutaneous alternating current stimulation.

Recording electrodes had platinum iridium shanks with a tip width of 125μm, an impedance of 0.4 MOhm, and were spaced 0.5mm apart in the lateral plane. An additional shank with 10 kOhm impedance served as a reference. Electrodes were inserted ~300μm into the craniotomy exposed sensorimotor cortex (L2/3). Data was acquired with a sampling rate of 24 kHz (Tucker-Davis Technologies).

Stimulation electrodes were adhered directly to the skull, medial and lateral the cranial window.



There are two data files.

The first contains experiments where 9 three-month old Sprague-Dawley rats received a randomized order of stimulation parameters (frequencies: 5, 10, 20, 40, and 140 Hz, amplitudes: 50, 100, 200, and 400 μA). Each dated folder contains recordings for a single rat, with subfolders for each stimulation parameter delivery (1-min baseline + 1-min sACS + 1-min baseline).

The second data file contains experiments where 13 rats received a single 20-min delivery of 40 Hz, 100 μA stimulation. Each dated folder contains experiments from a single rat. Baseline activity was recorded for 20-min before stimulation onset and subsequently following sACS for 10 minutes. Additional subfolders contain further 20-min of post stimulation baseline activity, collected to an hour following stimulation offset.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Transcranial Focused Ultrasound Stimulation Targeting White Matter Inhibits Seizures in a Rat Model of Epilepsy
dandisets.description: In this dataset, we recorded 32-channel EEG and 64-channel intracranial signals from wild-type male rats and male rats with seizures induced by repeated 4-AP injections to investigate the effects of white-matter-targeted transcranial focused ultrasound (tFUS) on seizure inhibition. The 4-AP cocktail was injected into the right S1, and intracranial recordings were obtained from the left S1. The "labeled data" include seizure onset and offset times for each seizure, which were used to compare the tFUS and sham (no tFUS) conditions for each rat. Each rat has data from 4–5 sessions, each lasting approximately one hour: BASELINE (pre-tFUS or pre-sham), condition (CC_1Hz or sham), post1 (first hour post-stimulation), post2 (second hour post-stimulation), and post3 (third hour post-stimulation).tFUS was delivered using a 128-element transducer targeting the corpus callosum (CC, predominantly white matter) with a 1 Hz pulse repetition frequency (PRF), 2% duty cycle (DC), and ~127 kPa peak pressure. The "EP data" contain evoked potentials used to verify accurate targeting of the stimulation site with 1500Hz PRF, 30% DC and 67ms ultrasound duration (UD). The "EEG data for PLV" includes the EEG data for connectivity analysis of wild-type rats. Detailed description of this dataset can be found in the following publication. Please cite the paper if you would use a portion of the dataset. H. Gao, A. Frake, D. M. Durand and B. He, "Transcranial Focused Ultrasound Stimulation Targeting White Matter Inhibits Seizures in a Rat Model of Epilepsy," in IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering, vol. 34, pp. 251-259, 2026, doi: 10.1109/TNSRE.2025.3644273
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Data related to "Xiao, Severson, et al. (2026) Peri-Head Distance Coding in the Mouse Brainstem"
dandisets.description:
This Dandiset contains in vivo extracellular recordings from second-order neurons in the mouse whisker brainstem (principal trigeminal nucleus - PrV) collected in awake animals during a naturalistic wall-passing paradigm designed to probe peri-head distance sensing.  The dataset supports analysis of two distance-coding schemes observed in the brainstem: 
(1) a proximity code, in which firing rates increase monotonically as an object approaches the face, and 
(2) a map code, in which neurons exhibit peak tuning at specific peri-head distances, collectively tiling peri-personal space. 
Population-level analyses of these recordings can be used to compare decoding performance between coding schemes. In addition to baseline recordings, the dataset includes perturbation conditions intended to test circuit mechanisms underlying map-like tuning, including multi-whisker integration, local inhibition, and long-range internuclear inhibition.  
Data are organized in NWB format and include extracellular electrophysiology and associated stimulus annotations needed to relate neural activity to peri-head distance during wall-passing (e.g., wall/object position relative to the head and temporal alignment to ephys recording).  
Figure reproduction assets are available in the associated Github repository (https://github.com/wanglab-neuro/2026_Xiao-Severson_Peri-head-distance), under "Manuscript figures". 
Experimental notes are available at https://www.brainstem.org/public/stem/project/1ae87b47-5c85-42fd-9341-919aa220d647/.

dandisets.title: EMUsort Rat Datasets (Acquired): high-resolution multichannel EMG recordings with 3D motion tracking during treadmill locomotion
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains 3 high-resolution EMG recordings (16-channel bipolar) from rat triceps brachii along with synchronized 3D motion tracking for 10 keypoints during treadmill locomotion. The motion tracking was acquired at 125Hz using 4 BlackFly S cameras (BFS-U3-16S2M-CS) and labeled with DeepLabCut. The calibration and triangulation was performed with Anipose and this Dandiset contains the 3D points outputted in the pose-3d folder, along with the confidence values. A small proportion of these values (outliers) were interpolated by the cubic spline method if they had a confidence below 0.8 or if crossing a jump threshold of 6 pixels compared to a 7-frame median-filtered history (i.e., score_threshold=0.8, medfilt=7, offset_threshold=6, see Anipose documentation). The interpolation of a time point is represented by a NaN value for the confidence score of a given keypoint. No further filtering was performed on the 3D pose estimates. Along with these, this Dandiset also contains labels for the initial contact and initial swing times for the forelimb palm of the implanted side (left; i.e., palm_L).
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Assessing Chance in Neuro-Vascular Interactions
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains invasive neurophysiology and tissue oxygen recordings collected in awake, restrained rabbits during 10-minute resting-state sessions, supporting the analyses in “Assessing Chance in Neuro-Vascular Interactions.” The dataset is intended for studying neurovascular interactions/vasomotion and for reproducing the surrogate-based statistical framework used in the manuscript. This dandiset contains data from 43 resting-state experiments (10 minutes each) consisting of simultaneous brain tissue PO₂ recordings and neuronal activity measures (LFP band envelopes and/or single-unit activity). Experiments were performed in 11 pigmented Dutch-belted rabbits; see the associated manuscript for subject details and experimental context.

dandisets.title: An anatomical hotspot for striatal dopamine-acetylcholine interactions during reward and movement: multi-fiber photometry data
dandisets.description: Multi-fiber photometry data in Bouabid, Vu, et al (2026) "An anatomical hotspot for striatal dopamine-acetylcholine interactions during reward and movement": Dopamine (DA) and acetylcholine (ACh) are key neuromodulators that regulate striatal circuits underlying movement and reinforcement learning. Evidence suggests that DA and ACh systems interact, but where and how interactions are expressed across striatal regions in behaviorally relevant release dynamics remains unknown. We applied micro-fiber arrays to simultaneously measure striatum-wide DA and ACh in behaving mice, revealing an anatomical organization in which DA-ACh anti-correlations were concentrated in a hotspot in the anterior dorsolateral striatum (aDLS). Anti-correlations resulted from temporally coincident pairs of spontaneous and event-locked transient peak and dip events occurring in a DA-to-ACh sequence. The aDLS localized hotspot was consistently expressed within distinct signals associated with unpredicted rewards, learned and extinguished Pavlovian cues, and locomotion initiation and invigoration phases, for which we revealed novel, opposing DA-ACh dynamics. Optogenetic activation of DA neurons selectively suppressed spontaneous ACh release within the aDLS hotspot, and ex vivo recordings revealed enhanced D2-mediated ACh inhibition in aDLS relative to ventral regions, suggesting a mechanistic basis for this spatial specificity. These findings demonstrate that DA-ACh interactions during behavior are spatially organized, rather than uniformly conserved, and shape behaviorally relevant dynamics to regulate region-specific functions in learning and movement control.

dandisets.title: High-density extracellular recordings (Neuropixels/SiNAPS) of Hippocampal-Cortical dynamics during spatial behavior.
dandisets.description:
Overview This dataset provides high-density, simultaneous extracellular recordings from the mouse hippocampal formation (DG, CA1, CA2, CA3) and the retrosplenial cortex (RSC). The data captures large-scale neural dynamics across homecage rest, spatial navigation, and sensory stimulation.

Methodology:
Probes: Recordings were performed using Neuropixels 2.0 (CA3, CA1, RSC) and 1024-channel SiNAPS probes (DG, CA3, CA2, CA1).
Behavioral Paradigms: Neuropixels sessions include PRE-rest, Maze navigation (Novel or 2-maze configurations), and POST-rest homecage recordings. A subset of animals includes head-fixed odor stimulation.
SiNAPS sessions include High-density head-fixed recordings.

Data Included:
Sorted isolated units (Spikes).
Local Field Potentials (LFP), specifically including single-channel traces from ripple-detection sites.
Behavioral tracking variables (position, velocity, sleep periods, sharp-wave ripple times).
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: DANDI Compute
dandisets.description: This is an experiment in reproducible computing related to the DANDI Archive.  Check back in a few months for results - more details to follow! The outer level of the Dandiset is organized according to a BIDS-Study layout.  The primary `derivatives/` directory follows the Type 1 BIDS-Derivatives structure. See the linked GitHub companion repository for more information!
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Loss of oxytocin receptor function disrupts neural signatures of pair bonding and fidelity in the nucleus accumbens
dandisets.description: The formation of enduring relationships dramatically influences future behavior, promoting affiliation between familiar individuals. The neural mechanisms of how such attachments are encoded to elicit and reinforce specific social behaviors in distinct ethological contexts remain understudied. Signaling via the oxytocin receptor (Oxtr) in the nucleus accumbens (NAc) facilitates social reward as well as pair bond formation between mates in socially monogamous prairie voles. How Oxtr function influences activity in the NAc during pair bonding to promote affiliative behavior with partners and rejection of other potential mates has not been determined. Using in vivo fiber photometry across pair bond formation and social attachment behaviors, we demonstrate that genetic loss of Oxtr function sex-specifically disrupts pair bonding behaviors and associated activity in the NAc. In females, loss of Oxtr influences prosocial behavior in a bonding-state-dependent manner. Oxtr-null females demonstrate reduced prosocial behaviors and lower activity in the NAc during initial chemosensory investigation of novel males. Following bond formation, affiliative behavior with partners and neural activity in the NAc during these interactions increase in an Oxtr-independent manner. Conversely, males lacking Oxtr display increased prosocial investigation of novel females. Altered behavioral and neural activity patterns in Oxtr-null males during their first interactions with females predict their future preference for a partner or stranger days later. These findings reconcile prior pharmacological and genetic studies. We demonstrate that disrupting Oxtr function sex-specifically influences the early development of pair bonds by modulating prosociality and the neural processing of sensory cues and social interactions with novel individuals, unmasking underlying sex differences in the neural pathways regulating the formation of long-term relationships.

dandisets.title: Aery Jones et al (2026) Entorhinal cortex represents task-relevant remote locations independent of CA1
dandisets.description: This dataset contains Neuropixels recordings from medial entorhinal cortex and hippocampus while mice learned a spatial match-to-sample task. Data includes spike times, LFP from select channels, trial information, 2D position, and head direction.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Hippocampal CA2 Perineuronal Net Degradation and Social Memory Deficits in a 5XFAD Alzheimer’s Disease Mouse Model
dandisets.description: This dataset contains raw and processed experimental data supporting the findings reported in Chaunsali et al. (2025), Alzheimer’s & Dementia, DOI:10.1002/alz.70813. The study investigates the disruption of perineuronal nets (PNNs) in the hippocampal CA2 region of the 5XFAD Alzheimer’s disease mouse model and its association with selective loss of social cognition memory. Included are: Immunohistochemical imaging data quantifying PNN integrity in CA2 neurons, behavioral assay results assessing social memory performance and bulk RNA-sequencing data from hippocampal CA2 tissue with associated metadata.These data provide mechanistic insights into how PNN degradation correlates with specific forms of memory loss in neurodegenerative disease models and can be reused for comparative studies of extracellular matrix remodeling, Alzheimer’s disease pathology, and memory circuits. All data have been organized in compliance with FAIR principles to support reuse in neuroscience research and NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy requirements. Transcriptomic data associated with this study are deposited separately in the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) under accession GSE317499 and are linked here as a related resource. Transcriptomic data associated with this study are deposited separately in the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) under accession GSE317499 and are linked here as a related resource.

The study employs a decision-making task involving sensory perception to understand the neural basis of altered prior utilization.

dandisets.title: Electrophysiological Dataset of Patch-seq Recording in non-Human Primate Anterior Insular Cortex

Using Patch-seq, we integrated electrophysiological, morphological, and transcriptomic profiling at the single-cell level in acute brain slices. The dataset primarily comprises electrophysiological recordings annotated with transcriptomic cell types as metadata.

dandisets.title: Data supporting "Cortical ensembles orchestrate social competition through hypothalamic outputs" (Padilla-Correano, et al., Nature 2022)
dandisets.description: This dataset contains in vivo electrophysiological recordings and simultaneous behavioral data from male mice in NWB (Neurodata Without Borders) format.   Recordings were obtained during a reward competition task.  See the publication for protocol details:  Padilla-Coreano, N., Batra, K., Patarino, M. et al. Cortical ensembles orchestrate social competition through hypothalamic outputs. Nature 603, 667–671 (2022).  https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-022-04507-5.   Electrophysiological neural recordings were taken in the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC), mPFC projections to BLA, mPFC projections to LH.  The mPFC recordings contain "ephys" in filename; the mPFC to BLA contain “BLA” in file name, and mPFC to LH contain “chrimson” in the filename. Animals followed a consistent dye pattern across cages of 4 mice to identify them in behavioral videos.  Animal 1 was dyed on left side of body.  Animal 2 was dyed on right side of body.  Animal 3 was dyed with three horizontal stripes along body.  Animal 4 was dyed around the neck only.   Note that the animal id is different from animal social rank; please see subject description for social rank information. Raw behavioral videos are also included for the BLA and chrimson files.  Animal pose tracking was performed with AlphaTracker and required manual  correction of animal identities. Identity-corrected AlphaTracker pose estimates are included in this dataset.  Additionally, nine categories of  animal behavioral data, as described in Figure 1f of the reference paper, was hand scored for each trial from -2 s before tone onset to +12 seconds  after tone onset.  Note that there is an offset between the start of the neural recording and the start of the behavioral videos; this information  in contained in the video_gap variable in the behavioral events.  In the behavioral videos, the LED is on whenever the CS-tone is played and can be  used to verify the correct alignment between neural and behavioral data. Metadata includes approximate age, sex, species, strain, social rankings  of each animal and competitor (assessed with tube test), and port entries during reward competition task.  Not every subject in the dataset has  their competitor’s session included; those that do are noted in the subject description.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Electrophysiological Dataset of Somatic Sodium Channel Current Using Outside-out Recording in the Primate Anterior Insular Cortex
dandisets.description: This dataset includes intracellular recordings from neurons in the anterior insular cortex of macaque monkeys. Somatic sodium channel currents were recorded using the outside-out patch-clamp configuration in neurons from crab-eating monkeys.

dandisets.title: Electrophysiological Dataset of Local Neuronal Microcircuits in the Primate Anterior Insular Cortex
dandisets.description: This dataset includes intracellular recordings from neurons in the anterior insular cortex of macaque monkeys. Neuronal connectivity was assessed using simultaneous multi–patch-clamp recordings in crab-eating monkeys.

dandisets.title: Electrophysiological Dataset of Neuronal Post-Synaptic Currents in the Primate Anterior Insular Cortex
dandisets.description: This dataset includes intracellular recordings from neurons in the anterior insular cortex of macaque monkeys. Postsynaptic currents were measured, comprising miniature and spontaneous excitatory and inhibitory postsynaptic currents (E/IPSCs) recorded from von Economo neurons (VENs) and pyramidal cells (PCs).

dandisets.title: 20260220_AIBS_Patchseq_BICAN_HMBA_macaque
dandisets.description: BICAN HMBA Lein PatchSeq upload (nonhuman primate) (Q1 2026)

dandisets.title: Three-dimensional spatial selectivity of hippocampal neurons during space flight
dandisets.description: This dataset contains extracellular recordings from hippocampal place cells in three freely moving rats during the Neurolab Space Shuttle mission (STS-90) in April 1998. The experiment investigated whether the hippocampus can maintain stable spatial representations during three-dimensional navigation in microgravity. Rats were implanted with multi-electrode recording arrays and trained to traverse a three-dimensional track called the "Escher staircase," in which three 90-degree yaw turns were interleaved with three 90-degree pitch turns, creating a closed loop after only 270 degrees of total yaw rotation. This design placed the spatial information from external landmarks in conflict with the expected input from the head-direction system, which normally requires 360 degrees of yaw to signal a return to the starting direction. The dataset includes both preflight recordings on a flat rectangular track and in-flight recordings on the Escher staircase track from flight days 4 and 9. Place cells eventually demonstrated normal spatially specific firing, with spatial information content comparable to preflight levels, though initial exposures on flight day 4 showed abnormal spatial selectivity patterns. Hippocampal EEG was also recorded, showing normal theta rhythm during locomotion and normal sharp waves and ripples during quiet rest. Rats received medial forebrain bundle stimulation as a reward for traversing the track.

dandisets.title: Neuropixels recordings in anesthetized rats (saline vs. LSD) with intracranial electrical stimulation
dandisets.description:
Neuropixels 1.0 recordings from medial prefrontal cortex in seven anesthetized Sprague-Dawley rats (3 LSD, 4 saline), 24 hours after administration of 0.2 mg/kg LSD or saline (vehicle). During recording, electrical stimulation was delivered to the basolateral amygdala (BLA). Raw electrophysiology (AP 30 kHz, LFP 2.5 kHz) and NIDQ auxiliary channels with stimulation triggers are provided per subject under sourcedata/ as NWB, with BIDS/BEP032 sidecars (probe, channels, electrodes, coordinate system). Kilosort4 spike sorting and bombcell quality metrics are provided as derivatives for subject NP06.

Electrode coordinates use the Paxinos & Watson atlas in standard stereotaxic convention (mm; AP and ML relative to bregma, DV relative to brain surface): x = anterior-posterior (anterior positive), y = medial-lateral (right positive), z = dorsal-ventral (dorsal positive, ventral negative). Probe insertion: AP +3.4 mm, ML 0.75 mm (left hemisphere), DV -6.0 mm (probe tip), perpendicular (0 deg). BLA stimulation site: AP -1.7, ML 4.5, DV -7.5 mm.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Photometry data underlying the analyses in Chantranupong PMID: 37557915
dandisets.description: The photometry and associated behavioral data recorded in the striatum of mice performing a freely moving water-motivated 2-armed bandit task.  Recordings of acetylcholine, dopamine, and GCAMP.  Many simultaneous Ach and DA recordings.

dandisets.title: OpenScope Predictive Processing Community - Mesoscope
dandisets.description: This dataset contains data acquired on the Mesoscope platform as part of the OpenScope predictive processing community project

dandisets.title: NIH BRAIN CONNECTS - LINC UM1 - Human Data - Scratch
dandisets.description: This Dandiset is meant to host scratch data for the LINC project. Once the data in this Dandiset are ready to be published, they will be manually moved over to Dandiset 1278 (https://dandiarchive.org/dandiset/001278).

dandisets.title: Chronic Neuropixels recordings with 3D pose estimation via DANNCE during spontaneous exploration
dandisets.description: This dataset contains (1) Kilosorted data from chronically implanted Neuropixels probe(s), (2) video data from 6 Basler cameras with synchronized frames, (3) 3D keypoint positions of 20 keypoints across the entire body as estimated by DANNCE. This data is collected from a Long Evans rat as she spontaneously explores a ~1m diameter open arena. Data collected in Bence Ölveckzy's laboratory.

dandisets.title: Fujimoto et al., Nat. Neurosci. 2026, Deep brain stimulation induces white matter remodeling and functional changes to brain-wide networks
dandisets.description: DOI: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41593-026-02301-4 Data and code (fMRI, DWI IF, EM, behavior)

dandisets.title: Mouse hippocampal electrophysiology during spatial working memory and consummatory behavior
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains mouse hippocampal dorsal CA1 electrophysiology and behavioral data acquired during delayed non-match-to-place (DNMP), open-field, and related foraging experiments. Data include sorted spike times (using MountainSort4), LFP, position tracking, trial intervals, consummatory and other behavioral event annotations.

dandisets.title: Two-photon calcium imaging of responses to drifting gratings and natural movies in the mouse visual cortex and Spatial transcriptomics data
dandisets.description: This dataset contains recordings of neural activity using two-photon calcium imaging from the mouse primary visual cortex (VISp). The mice had GCaMP8s expressed in their inhibitory interneurons (Slc32a1). The experiments were conducted using the same data collection pipeline as the Allen Brain Observatory (de Vries et al., Nature Neurosci., 2020), with an improved microscopy system that uses multi-plane imaging. A running wheel was used to track locomotion. The recordings were performed during two different visual stimulus sets: 1. STAGE_0: Natural movie clips. 2. STAGE_1: Drifting gratings presented as full-field stimuli (approximately 120x90 degrees of visual space). These gratings were presented in 8 directions, at 5 contrasts (5%, 10%, 20%, 40%, 80%), as well as 8 directions, and 5 temporal frequencies (0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8 Hz). This dataset also includes the gene expression tables associated with subsets of neurons in the neural recording dataset. The gene expression was quantified using HCR (Hybridization Chain Reaction) method and multiple rounds of multichannel lightsheet imaging. Transcriptomic data in available in a Github repository: https://github.com/omidzobeiri/CellxGene_tables_U01_130907_DS001778

dandisets.title: Cholinergic interneuron control of GABAergic circuits targeting spiny projection neurons is disrupted in parkinsonian models
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains all raw and primary-processed data underlying the study 'Cholinergic interneuron control of intrastriatal GABAergic circuits targeting spiny projection neurons is disrupted in Parkinson’s disease models'. Parkinson’s disease (PD) is known to alter the intrinsic properties of striatal cholinergic interneurons (ChIs). However, how PD shapes ChI control of intrastriatal GABAergic circuits regulating principal spiny projection neurons (SPNs) is unknown. To fill this gap, striatal circuits in healthy and parkinsonian mice were interrogated. In ex vivo brain slices from healthy mice, optogenetic stimulation of ChIs evoked GABAA receptor currents in both indirect and direct pathway SPNs that were attributable to nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR)-mediated activation of GABAergic interneurons. Simulations suggested that this circuit exerts a state-dependent control of SPN dendritic integration that was modulated by concomitant muscarinic receptor signaling. Surprisingly, in mouse models of prodromal and parkinsonian states, the ability of ChIs to engage this intrastriatal circuitry was disrupted because interneurons down-regulated nicotinic AChRs. Taken together, these studies suggest that impaired ChI control of GABAergic interneurons contribute to behavioral deficits in both prodromal and clinical PD states.

dandisets.title: CYP2C11 Overexpression Targeting Peri-lesion Astrocytes to Improve Cognitive Function in Mice with Multi-infarct Dementia
dandisets.description: CYP2C11 is a key generating enzyme of epoxyeicosatrienoic acids (EETs). Localized EET production will promote vasodilation, increase cerebral blood flow (CBF) in hypoperfused regions, and suppress neuroinflammation, thereby potentially improving cognitive function. Thus, AAV-mediated astrocyte overexpression of CYP2C11 may provide therapeutic benefit for a large patient population with a significant unmet medical need of dementia therapy.

dandisets.title: Naturalistic behavior and self-generated neural activity predictive of self-correction
dandisets.description: Data for the paper "Naturalistic behavior and self-generated neural activity predictive of self-correction" by Shijie Gu, Chenyan Liu, Anna K. Gillespie, Rhino Nevers, Eric L. Denovellis, Michael E. Coulter, Kenny Kay, and Loren M. Frank Hippocampus recording during rats changing their minds. Paper abstract: Changing one’s mind –– revising a past decision and independently of external cues –– can lead to better outcomes, but its neural basis remains poorly understood. We developed a challenging spatial task for rats where the conflict between task rules and innate biases leads to abundant, spontaneous, and characteristically corrective changes-of-mind (COMs). Neural recordings reveal a striking two-stage organization underlying COMs. In hippocampus, a brain region implicated in counterfactual thinking, spatial representations bifurcated around COM onset: representations of distant alternatives emerged only after animals began to change course. These representations predicted their eventual choice, and often serially encoded trial start and end locations. Our task paradigm reveals that the brain processes underlying self-correction are dissociable and uncovers a rich repertoire of hippocampal spatial representations tied to behavior.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Global/Local Oddball project macaque monkey multi-area neurophysiology
dandisets.description: This dataset was collected for the Global Local Oddball project, in collaboration with the Allen Institute's OpenScope project. This dataset is collected from macaque monkeys. The brain rapidly adapts to unchanging environments and is most excited by surprising events. A leading model to explain this phenomenon is predictive coding. Predictive coding proposes that the brain compares incoming sensory information against a prediction signal. This prediction is based on an internal model that is generated in higher-order cortex. This model reflects the brain’s assumptions about the statistics of the environment. If an incoming sensory signal matches the prediction, the two signals cancel. Expected sensory data thus are “explained away” and leave the brain unexcited. In other words, predictive coding is subtractive. Whenever the sensory signal does not match the prediction, subtraction results in a larger value, called the prediction error. This error signal initiates excitation in the lower-order cortex that propagates feedforward up the cortical hierarchy. Prediction errors then instigate updates to the internal model to improve future predictions. Associated prediction update signals flow back down the hierarchy. Consistent with this model, optogenetic silencing of top-down inputs from frontal to visual cortex largely eliminates prediction error signals. However, the precise circuit mechanisms that generate these signals are largely unknown. Specifically, by recording from multiple laminar high-density probes across the visual cortical hierarchy, we aimed to uncover what information is carried by layer 2 and 3 spikes that feed forward vs. by layer 5 and 6 spikes that feed back, using recently established analytic tools.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Cooperative Behavior in WT and Fmr1 Rat Dyads
dandisets.description: This Dandiset contains behavioral data from wild-type and Fmr1 knockout rat dyads performing a novel spatial cooperation task designed to investigate the behavioral strategies and social mechanisms underlying cooperative behavior. In this task, rat pairs learn to coordinate visits to matching reward wells on paired spatial mazes under deterministic and probabilistic reward contingencies. The dataset includes behavioral event timestamps, reward well visit intervals, and position tracking data for both animals within each dyad across multiple sessions (N = 5 pairs per genotype group). These data support analyses of cooperative performance, leader–follower dynamics, behavioral synchrony, and adaptive coordination strategies relevant to social cognition and neurodevelopmental disorders. Related findings from this dataset are described in: Shukla A, Rivera EL, Bladon JH, Jadhav SP (2026) A novel cooperative behavior assay in rats reveals distinct social coordination strategies of increasing complexity and social reciprocity deficits in a model of Fragile-X Syndrome. Journal of Neuroscience, e0143262026. https://doi.org/10.1523/JNEUROSCI.0143-26.2026. This work was supported by the SFARI Autism Rat Models Consortium Grant SFA-AN-AR-Rat Models-00012700 to S.P.J.
keywords: Cooperative behavior; Social coordination; Social reciprocity; Fragile X syndrome; Autism spectrum disorder; Social decision-making; Social cognition; Rat model; Behavioral synchrony; Cooperative learning; Leader–follower dynamics; Neurodevelopmental disorders

dandisets.title: 3D µEFPocket for Multimodal Communications with Brain Organoids
dandisets.description: Human brain organoids are powerful three-dimensional models for studying neural development, disease mechanisms, and pharmacological responses, but precise spatiotemporal chemical modulation and activity recording within intact organoids and in a simultaneous fashion is difficult due to their three-dimensional (3D) complexities. Here, we introduce the microElectroFluidicPocket (µEFPocket), a conformal, ultrathin microfluidic 3D biointerface that integrates microscale spatiotemporal control of chemical and electrical stimulation in human cortical organoids with simultaneous optical and electrical readout. Fabricated entirely from parylene C using standard wafer-scale deposition, photolithography, and etching, µEFPockets form integrated, mechanically compliant architectures that wrap around spherical organoids without attachment factors, enabling repeated use of the same organoid. Engineered and independently addressable microchannels enable localized, time-locked delivery of neurotransmitters with hundreds-micron spatial resolution and second-level temporal control while preserving global culture conditions. Using glutamate delivery or electrical stimulation of GCaMP-expressing human cortical organoids, we demonstrate spatially localized calcium responses at regional and single-neuron resolution, revealing network propagation and stimulus-dependent adaptation. This scalable platform provides a foundation for spatially patterned pharmacology, multimodal biointerfaces, and closed-loop functional assays in organoids.
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Chronic electrophysiology and two-photon calcium imaging of mouse primary somatosensory cortex during intracortical microstimulation learning
dandisets.description:
Processed electrophysiology and two-photon calcium imaging data from chronic recordings of mouse primary somatosensory cortex (S1, trunk region) during an intracortical microstimulation (ICMS)–cued wheel-turn detection task, acquired longitudinally across weeks of learning. ICMS was delivered through chronically implanted 32-channel ultraflexible nanoelectronic thread (NET) probes co-implanted with a cranial window in Thy1-GCaMP6s mice, enabling simultaneous sorted single-unit electrophysiology and volumetric/planar two-photon imaging.

The dataset comprises 12 mice: 6 task-trained animals (simultaneous electrophysiology and two-photon imaging) and 6 passive controls (survey two-photon activation mapping; four also with electrophysiology) across 85 sessions. Each NWB file contains sorted units (spike times and mean waveforms), electrode and stimulation metadata, behavioral trials and ICMS stimulation events, a wheel-encoder signal, and processed imaging (DF/F traces with ROI segmentations for task animals; per-neuron activation across stimulation channel and current for controls). Data accompany Kim et al. 2026 (Luan Lab, Rice University).

dandisets.title: Data for fly navigational responses exploiting plume-specific odor motion and gradient cues
dandisets.description:
This Dandiset contains data underlying Brudner, Zhou, et al., "Fly navigational responses exploit plume-specific odor motion and gradient cues." It includes both walking-navigation behavior NWB assets and supplemental odor-plume stimulus/figure data used in the paper. The behavioral assets contain adult Drosophila melanogaster trajectories from flies responding to two structurally distinct virtual odor plumes, a spatially smooth plume and an intermittent complex plume, delivered optogenetically (CsChrimson) in a laminar-air arena. For each fly trajectory, the behavioral NWB files provide tracked 2-D position and per-frame derived signals, plus a table of detected saccade-like turning events. Data collection and analysis methods are described in the Methods section of the associated Brudner, Zhou, et al. paper; no separate protocols.io protocol URL is available at this time.

The supplemental plume data include freestream and complex-plume movie/figure assets. The derived freestream plot-input asset, sub-figs1-freestream-plumes/sub-figs1-freestream-plumes_ses-figure-s1_behavior.nwb, contains compact data used to render the Supplementary Figure 1 freestream plume panels: example frames, mean vertical-gradient maps, mean vertical odor-motion cue maps, exact plotted frequency/intermittency scatter points, panel color scales, and provenance/timing metadata for the 5, 10, and 20 cm/s freestream plumes.

The Dandiset additionally includes standardized 1500-frame movie windows used by the Figure S1 workflow. Freestream image assets are provided one per plume: sub-figs1-freestream-a0001/sub-figs1-freestream-a0001_ses-figure-s1_image.nwb for the 5 cm/s freestream plume, sub-figs1-freestream-a0002/sub-figs1-freestream-a0002_ses-figure-s1_image.nwb for the 10 cm/s freestream plume, and sub-figs1-freestream-a0003/sub-figs1-freestream-a0003_ses-figure-s1_image.nwb for the 20 cm/s freestream plume. Each freestream image asset stores only the 25 s standardized window used by the analysis and figure workflow: 1500 frames at 60 Hz, axes time,y,x, pixel size 0.153 mm/px, corresponding to raw H5 time 16.0-41.0 s after skipping 240 native 15 Hz frames. The full 4-minute raw H5 plume movies are not included. The freestream plume measurements underlying these assets were collected/quantified by Connor et al. in Connor, McHugh & Crimaldi, "Quantification of airborne odor plumes using planar laser-induced fluorescence" (Experiments in Fluids 59:137, 2018, https://doi.org/10.1007/s00348-018-2591-3).

The complex smoke plume movie window is provided as sub-figs1-complex-smoke-1a/sub-figs1-complex-smoke-1a_ses-figure-s1_image.nwb. This asset stores the background-subtracted/preprocessed smoke_1a_orig analysis movie: 1500 frames at 60 Hz, axes time,y,x, pixel size 0.153 mm/px, corresponding to raw AVI frames 300-1799 (5.0-30.0 s). The smoke_1a_orig complex plume was analyzed throughout the paper, not only for Supplementary Figure 1. The full 3600-frame raw smoke_1a_orig AVI is not included.

The smooth plume analyzed in the paper is not duplicated in this Dandiset. It is available through the Dryad dataset from Álvarez-Salvado et al., "Data from: Elementary sensory-motor transformations underlying olfactory navigation in walking fruit flies" (https://doi.org/10.5061/dryad.g27mq71), which includes the plume measurements in air. Those smooth-plume measurements were collected/quantified by Connor et al. in Connor, McHugh & Crimaldi, "Quantification of airborne odor plumes using planar laser-induced fluorescence" (Experiments in Fluids 59:137, 2018, https://doi.org/10.1007/s00348-018-2591-3).

dandisets.title: The Statistical Analysis of Brain Organoid MEA Data
dandisets.description:
Advances in stem cell culture and bioengineering have enabled the generation of brain organoids from induced pluripotent stem cells. These three-dimensional (3D) structures, composed of thousands to millions of cells, mimic key features of human brain organization. Brain organoids are increasingly used to study neurodevelopment, model neurological disease (e.g., schizophrenia and bipolar disorder), and explore emerging biocomputing applications. A central focus of these studies is neurophysiological electrical activity, typically recorded over several minutes using multi-electrode arrays designed for in vitro systems. Statistical analysis of these data is challenging due to substantial noise and the need to characterize spatial and temporal structure of organoid activity. Statistical methods therefore play an essential role in separating signal from noise and in identifying temporal and spatial components and their interaction. In this manuscript, we review the analysis of brain organoid electrophysiology data, from raw data acquisition through current processing and analysis pipelines, and we highlight limitations of existing methods as well as opportunities for further statistical development.
This dataset includes recordings from a 24-well MEA chip (MaxTwo).
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique

dandisets.title: Multimodal characterization of sensorimotor peripheral neuropathy in a preclinical mouse model of SCA3
dandisets.description: Spinocerebellar ataxia type 3 (SCA3), also known as Machado Joseph disease, is the most common dominantly inherited ataxia caused by a toxic gain-of-function CAG expansion in ATXN3. Currently, no effective preventive or disease-modifying treatments exist for this uniformly fatal disorder. This progressive, multisystem disorder is typified by neuronal degeneration and demyelination of critical parts of the central nervous system. Additionally, more than half of individuals with SCA3 also exhibit peripheral neuropathy symptoms like reduced limb sensation or burning pains, significantly impacting quality of life and contributing to mobility impairments. However, exploration into cell types and disease mechanisms underlying the SCA3 peripheral nervous system abnormalities remains limited by lack of comprehensive SCA3 animal model characterizations. This study employs the transgenic full-length human mutant ATXN3 YACQ84 (Q84/Q84) mouse to assess progression of sensorimotor behaviors, nerve conduction abnormalities, and ultrastructural features of neuropathy. Longitudinal nerve conduction studies in Q84/Q84 mice show prominent sensorimotor nerve conduction velocity reductions and loss of sural nerve signal amplitudes. Behaviorally, Q84/Q84 mice display hypersensitivity to thermal stimuli and hyposensitivity to mechanical stimuli, with impairments in balance and coordination. Histological analysis of Q84/Q84 peripheral nerves reveals combined neuronal and glial pathology, characterized by loss of both large myelinated and small unmyelinated fibers, alongside demyelination pathologies. Transcriptomic analyses of Q84/Q84 sciatic nerves and dorsal root ganglia revealed widespread differential gene expression spanning diverse somatosensory pathways, with dorsal root ganglia tissue also displaying substantial alternative splicing dysregulation. Our results characterize significant peripheral neuropathy in a robust SCA3 mouse model, providing a strong foundation for future mechanistic and preclinical therapeutic studies of peripheral neuropathy in SCA3.

dandisets.title: Mesoscale two-photon voltage imaging of single  Layer 5 somata and their corresponding apical dendrites in mouse visual cortex

dandisets.title: Standardized behavioral segmentation datasets
dandisets.description: A growing, standardized collection of behavioral-segmentation datasets: recordings in which behavior has been parsed into a timeline of labeled segments (an ethogram), stored in a single common format so that behavior-analysis tools can be developed and benchmarked across datasets without a separate loader per dataset. Each file uses the ndx-ethogram NWB extension (an EthogramBouts table plus an Ethogram catalogue) with markerless pose estimation (ndx-pose) and source video where available. The collection spans unsupervised motif/syllable discovery (keypoint-MoSeq, B-SOiD, VAME, and others via the Mlost benchmark) and dense expert human annotation (CalMS21). These are curated, derived conversions of publicly available datasets redistributed under CC-BY 4.0 with attribution to the original authors (keypoint-MoSeq: doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10636983; Mlost: doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14382973; CalMS21: doi.org/10.22002/D1.1991).

dandisets.title: EEG recordings from a Grin2b+/- rat model of GRIN2B neurodevelopmental disorder: absence seizures and sleep-wake abnormalities
dandisets.description:
Chronic wireless EEG and EMG recordings from freely behaving Long-Evans rats carrying a heterozygous knockout of Grin2b (Grin2b+/-), the gene encoding the GluN2B subunit of N-methyl-D-aspartate receptors (NMDARs). Grin2b+/- rats display spike-and-wave discharges (SWDs, the electrographic correlate of absence seizures) at higher incidence and duration than wild-type littermates, alongside reduced REM sleep and altered NREM/wake distributions.
Recordings were acquired over 72-96 h using the TaiNi wireless multichannel recording system (Tainitec, UK) with 14-channel skull-surface EEG electrode grids and bilateral neck EMG electrodes, at 250.4 Hz. Two consecutive 24-h baseline windows (BL1, BL2) were extracted per animal. Automated pipelines classified sleep states (Wake, NREM, REM) in 5-s epochs and detected SWD seizure events. Data contribute to the SFARI Autism Rat Models Consortium (ARC). See Hristova et al., Epilepsia 2025 (doi:10.1111/epi.18606).
techniques: multi electrode extracellular electrophysiology recording technique
